title: db_sqlserver
preview: SQL Server uses snapshot isolation to provide consistent reads without blocking writers. Snapshot isolation works by using row versioning in tempdb. Readers access older versions of rows while writers continue to modify data.

title: db_sql
preview: SQL Server supports row versioning-based isolation levels, including snapshot isolation and read committed snapshot isolation (RCSI). title: db_mongodb_indexing
preview: MongoDB uses indexes to improve query performance by reducing the number of documents that must be scanned. The default index is created on the _id field, but developers can create additional single-field, compound, multikey, text, and geospatial indexes.

title: redis_intro
preview: Redis is an open-source, in-memory data store that is commonly used as a database, cache, and message broker. It stores data in memory rather than on disk, which allows for extremely fast read and write operations.

title: redis_basics
preview: Redis is an in-memory data store used for caching, fast data access, and real-time application features.
